reviews.feedback: This is good but could be a bit more flashy and dramatic. Let's make the specifics pop a little, like dropping the name of the band or the medical school. I also think the "way he makes music" could be a little more interesting, and make some in jokes about thrash / hardcore. Also include something about helping users and having empathy for end-users

You can list my title, staff engineer. You can also indicate that I mentor software engineers, lead the Developer Experience guild, and am responsible for source automation at the core of the product. Imagile can be more of a footnote. Replace "high-leverage engineering cultures" with "building culture with smart and efficient tooling and leveling up engineers across the organization".

You can include that I didn't like medical school. "output genuinely greater than the parts" is a little too generic. Make it flashier. Replace "IC and technical leader" with "Staff Engineer" which should say both effectively. "It's what the solution actually does ... for the user". The About section is a bit too verbose and should be tighter and more organized, with less paragraphs but with more detail.

"IC and technical leader" isn't good.
"something interesting in health ech" is boring. "Durable" culture is not interesting. Bringing the creativity and fun back into it.
I'm a "staff engineer". "I'm here to meet fun people that want to build great things - and people that have tough business problems to solve."
"IC and technical leader" > "Staff Engineer". Otherwise I like the intersection statement. I like restating "founder of Imagile" as "I also consult through my business, Imagile"
